name: mise-en-place (Polyglot Dev Tool Manager)

performance.notes: Rust-based; fast version switching and tool installation

agent_readiness.notes: Polyglot runtime and tool manager (successor to asdf, rtx). mise install node@22. mise use python@3.12. .mise.toml for project pinning (replaces .tool-versions). Supports: Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, Rust, Java, erlang, 500+ tools via asdf plugins. mise run (task runner replacing Makefile/just). Environment variable management: [env] section in .mise.toml. Automatic version switching per directory. Faster than asdf (Rust vs shell). GitHub Actions support.

gotchas: Replacing asdf: .tool-versions still supported but .mise.toml preferred
Shell hook required: eval '$(mise activate zsh)'
Task runner features still maturing vs mature alternatives
Some asdf plugins may not work perfectly
best_when: Polyglot teams needing unified tool version management
Projects needing per-directory automatic tool version switching
avoid_when: Single-language projects (nvm for Node, pyenv for Python simpler)
When team already standardized on asdf
summary: mise-en-place (formerly rtx) is the modern polyglot dev tool manager. mise install node@22 python@3.12 go@1.23. .mise.toml for project pinning. Automatic per-directory version switching. 500+ tools via asdf plugin compatibility. Built-in task runner (mise run). Replaces asdf/nvm/pyenv/rbenv with one fast Rust tool. eval '$(mise activate zsh)' in shell profile.
